lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, the gang is there except for Rachel and Ross, who both come storming in. Rachel is still going off about Ross's secret marriage.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I cannot believe that you didn't tell me that we are still married!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look I was going to tell you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When?! After the birth of our first secret child?! (To All) Ross didn't get the annulment; we are still married.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're kidding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (overdoing it) Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, maybe it wasn't my best decision. But I just couldn't face another failed marriage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let me just jump in and ask, at what point did you think this was a successful marriage?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, come on, if you think about it, it's actually kinda funny. (He laughs, and he laughs alone.) Okay, maybe it's best not to think about it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is inexcusable. I am shocked to my very core!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, I told her you already knew.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Another lie. You have a sickness!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, just for my own piece of mind, you're not married to anymore of us are ya?
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey and Monica are at the counter getting some more coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Hey, somebody left their keys. (Looks at them) Ooohh, to a Porsche! {Transcriber's note: Oh come on! Who would leave the keys to their Porsche behind? If I had a Porsche, I'd have the keys surgically attached to my hand!} Hey Gunther, these yours?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Yeah, that's what I drive. I make four bucks an hour, I saved up for 350 years!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Na-uh! (To everyone there) Hey did anybody lose their keys?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, why don't you put them in the lost and found?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's a lost and found? (Gunther sets the box up on the table.) My shoe! (Grabs it out of the box.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You left a shoe here?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I didn't realize until I got home. I wasn't gonna walk all the way back down here with one shoe! Y'know what? I'm gonna go find that guy's car and leave a note on the windshield. (Goes to do so.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh good, when he comes back for his keys, I'll be sure to give him your shoe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! Thanks. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (coming in from the bathroom) Oh, good, good, you guys are here! Listen, how would like to spend tomorrow taking care of three incredibly cute little puppies?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, what a fun day! That sounds great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (not enthused) Yeah, all right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well I'll bring them by tomorrow morning. Okay, and uh, by the way, they're not actually puppies, they're Frank and Alice's triplets. Okay, see ya! (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa! What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (stops) Please! Please! Please! Please! Oh please! Please! Please! Frank and Alice asked me to baby sit the triplets and I'm nervous 'cause I've never done that before by myself!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't worry about it Phoebe, we'll absolutely do it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm gonna pass. 'Cause I was kinda iffy when it was puppies.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Chandler, come on! It'll give us great practice for when-(realizes what she's about to say and changes)-people with babies come to visit.
The street down the block from Central Perk, Joey has found the Porsche and is writing the note.
Guy #1: Nice car!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's not mine.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (walking up) I love your car.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's (looks up and sees the woman) mine.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I bet it's fast.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too! Yeah. And comfortable. Do uh, do you like leather seats?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (checks in the window to make sure it has them) It's got 'em!
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe and Rachel are having tea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, I still have boxes here. I still have boxes at Ross's, and I have nowhere to live! Wow. I could so easily freak out right now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What about me? I just found out that Denise is leaving town for a while, I don't have a roommate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe-maybe I could be your roommate Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe you could be my roommate!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well there's an idea!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That would be great! Wait, how long is Denise gone for?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, she said she'd be back December 26th.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: December 26th, huh maybe she's Santa Clause.
Phoebe laughs, then stops to think about it. Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (deadpan) Oh look who it is, my husband. The apple of my eye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I got us a court date for tomorrow at 2:00 and I picked up all the forms. I'll take care of everything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well sure, if you say you're gonna take care of everything I have no reason to doubt you. Give me those forms! (Grabs them from him.) All right, now I'm gonna do this my way and I don't want to hear a peep out of you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay Rach, but...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op! You're peeping!
Ross grunts something and hands her the pen he was trying to hand her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! Y'know what, I just got-why? Why did you do this?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look I told you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't wanna hear "Three failed marriages!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, if you'd had two failed marriages, you'd understand!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know what? Thanks to you I'm half way there! Ugh! Oh! I am so mad! Ross, I don't think I have ever been this angry!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What about the time I said we were on a break?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh! (Stares at him.)
Monica and Rachel's, the next day, Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe are baby-sitting the triplets. They each have one baby.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pheebs, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (rapidly) I'm doing okay. I think it's going well. Do you think they're having fun? Am I talking to fast?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nope, sound like me. Pheebs, it's going great. Look at Chandler with little baby girl Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Little baby girl Chandler, where I have heard that before? Oh right, Coach Ruben. (Tries to get her to drink a little more from the bottle when he suddenly smells something. It's times like these I'm glad Smell-O-Vision hasn't been invented.) Do you know what Pheebs? When you're done over there, we kinda have a situation over here too. (Phoebe is changing hers.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Na-uh, no, we are all responsible for our own babies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See that's where I think that you're wrong. We've been playing these babies man for man; we should really be playing a zone defense.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just think that things would go a lot smoother if we each have our own zone. Phoebe, you can be in charge of wiping. And y'know Mon, you can be in charge of diapering and I can be in charge of looking how cute they are when they put their hands around... (He degrades into baby talk, but he means when they grab his finger.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That sounds really great, but maybe you should be in charge of wiping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I'm a rookie. I should not be in the end zone.
[Time lapse, they have set up a little assembly line for diaper changes. Phoebe wipes, Chandler adds the powder, begrudgingly, and Monica puts the diaper on.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so great! This is exactly how we set the plates at the restaurant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? (Checking the final diaper) Well this is not what I ordered.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey babies! Oh, I'm having the best morning. That uh, that Porsche I've got the keys too, still there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shocking! Since you still have the keys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You should see the treatment I get when I'm with that car! People are friendly; they-they wanna talk, and not just about the car! One guy gave me advice about my equity investments.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What equity investments?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The ones that got me the Porsche! Will you keep up! (Chandler wipes his forehead with a baby wipe, that might have been used. He drops it disgustedly.) But I figured, if-if people keep seeing me just standing there, they're gonna start to think that I don't own it. So I figured I'll wash it. Right? Monica, you got a bucket and some soap I can borrow?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, I got soap and sponges and rags and Carnuba wax and polishing compound.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't even have a car!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. But umm, one time there was this really dirty car in front of the building, so I washed it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And six others.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There you are.
The Porsche, Joey is finishing up washing the car and is talking to a guy about the car.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, she tops out at 130.
Guy #2: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And that's just in the city. I get her up to 160 when I take her upstate.
Guy #2: Really! You got a place upstate?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure!
Guy #2: Well, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, take it easy.

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: Hey! That's my car.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? Oh uh, oh just give me five more minutes with it.

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: What-what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh I-I uh, found the keys and now I'm just polishing her up.

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: But it's my car!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but it's my wax.

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: Listen, I-I-I don't come to this city much so I don't know if you're crazy or this is some kind of street theater, but could I have my keys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure. Here. (He hands them over.) I'll uh, save your parking spot.

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: I'm not coming back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: The Porsche Owner
lines.text: I live upstate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, so did I.
The guy gets in and drives off.
Monica and Rachel's, the triplets are all in their crib as Monica and Phoebe watch them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know why I was so nervous about this. And I don't know why Frank and Alice are always complaining. This is so easy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, two hours, a lifetime that's the same.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Check it out! Check it out! When the babies wake up, they can meet Krog! (He holds up this Xena-like warrior action figure.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, what are you doing? That thing can put someone's eye out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He can do more than that! He can destroy the universe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No Chandler, they can swallow one of those little parts! And also, look at his smooth area, that's just gonna mess them up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're not gonna swallow anything, you guys are being way over protective. When I was a kid, my mom used to just throw me into a pile of broken glass!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Glass, sand, whatever. (Walks out as Monica and Phoebe turn to check on the babies again.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, look at little Leslie stretching in her sleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh it's so cute. I wonder what age it is when you stop being able to put both legs over your head.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I can still do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How are you still single?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) All right. (Clears throat) I thought about it and maybe you're right. Maybe Krog is not a safe toy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good. What made you change your mind?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I swallowed the sonic blaster gun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How did that happen?!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I was trying to prove that I was right. Y'know? And it turns out I was wrong. And now it's lodged in my throat. (Mimics a cat trying to cough up a hairball.) (He does it again.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Damnit! Y'know this whole time we were concentrating on watching the babies and, and no one was watching Chandler! (He does it again.)
A judge's chambers, Rachel and Ross are filing their annulment papers.

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Okay you two are asking the court for an annulment?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes your honor, and here are, are forms, all filled out.

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: So based on your petition you are seeking an annulment on the grounds that Mr. Geller is mentally unstable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, I'm mentally unstable.

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: And based on the fact that Mr. Geller is intravenous drug user.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh yes, heroin and crack.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Crack isn't even an intravenous drug!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you would know.

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Now it also says here that you lied about your sexual preference before marriage?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starts to cry) Ross, please, I found the magazines!

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: And finally that you were unable to consummate the marriage. Well, that makes sense since you're gay and addicted to heroin.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'm sorry, this is insane! I-I-I'm not addicted to heroin, I'm not gay, and there is no problem with my ability to consummate anything! Look, I'll consummate this marriage right here, right now!

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: That won't be necessary.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And when we were dating we consummated like bunnies!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh!

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Now if you were two involved in a serious relationship, that really creates a problem.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! Your honor, rest assured relationship ended like two years ago! (To the stenographer) And could you strike "Consummated like bunnies" from the record?

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Is there, anything in this record that is actually true?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, yes, we got married in Vegas and uh, and the names I think.

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Well, based on what I heard, you two certainly don't qualify for an annulment. If you two don't want to be together you'll have to file for divorce.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stands up) That's great! Are you happy now? Look what you did with your funny, funny form!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stands up as well) What?! Me?! What about you and your consummated like bunnies nonsense!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And what-(notices the stenographer is still typing)-What are you typing that for? Did you hear what she said? We don't get the annulment. Don't type that! What?! Stop typing! (He goes over to where the stenographer is typing and in the process pushes Rachel out of his way.) Hey! Stop typing! (He's still typing.) Stop typing! Stop typing!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to the judge) Okay, do you see, do you see what you're keeping me married too?!

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: You need to get out of my chambers.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right look lady here is the deal, I came here for an annulment and I am not leaving here until I get one!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.text: Would you like to spend the night in jail?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And thank you for your time. (They both beat a hasty retreat.)
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is still doing the hairball thing as Monica and Phoebe are watching the babies. I can't describe it, you'll have to see it when it comes on in your area.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering wearing nothing but Porsche clothes) So the Porsche guy took his car back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you found the keys to his clothes?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. No, I just uh, I just loved the way it feels when everybody thinks I own a Porsche.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And people will think you own a Porsche because you're wearing the clothes?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course! Only an idiot would wear this stuff if you didn't have the car! Right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is true.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but only a genius would swallow a sonic blaster gun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I've been there. Yeah, I am gonna go drive my Porsche. (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, you know you don't actually have one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! What are you doing?! I'm in character! Would you talk to her! (Storms out.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, I think it just moved. It's really poking me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, that's it, we're going to the emergency room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! No, you can't, you can't leave me here with them! We're baby-sitting!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The babies are asleep, I'm sure you'll be okay on your own for a while!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But you-you can't leave me with them! We-we're a team! We're playing a zone! They're gonna triple team me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's got something plastic lodged in his throat, we've got to go to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But no, because a doctor won't be able to help him, it's just gonna y'know naturally pass through his system in like seven years.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think that's gum.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm pretty sure it's gun.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, listen this really hurts. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A real man wouldn't just run to the hospital! (They don't stop.) No! What would, what would Krog do? (They ignore her and Phoebe is left alone.)
The street, Joey is hanging out wearing his Porsche grab.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why isn't that valet back with my Porsche?

lines.speaker: Passerby
lines.text: Maybe because you've got the keys?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to women passing him) Porsche.
Ross and Rachel approach, they're still yelling at each other.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is totally your fault!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My fault?! You threatened the judge!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you ripped the paper out of the court reporter's machine!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was the only way I could get him to stop typing!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi! How are the Gellers?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't call us that! (Storms away)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The judge wouldn't let us get an annulment! Now we gotta get a divorce!! Did a Porsch throw up on you? (Walks on.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! It's Porsche!! (He's right y'know.)
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is being triple teamed.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me taking care of you is no problem, huh? You guys feel safe. Right? Okay, I'm gonna take that spit bubble as, "Yeah, I do!" Okay, after I get rid of this dirty diaper Leslie, I'll set you up with a clean one. (She throws it at the garbage without looking, misses, and knocks over a vase and flowers, which fall to the floor and break.) Okay, I'm sorry. I didn't mean to scare you. I just have to clean that up. Okay? 'Cause let's face it, we're at Monica's. (She crawls over, disposes of the diaper, picks up the flowers, and the vase.) I broke it. All right. Well, that's just the way that goes. (She throws out both the flowers and the vase.) Okay, good. (She turns around and only counts two babies.) Why are there only two of you? Where is Leslie? Well, you can't answer. (She starts looking for her) Leslie? Where are you Leslie? Leslie, now would be a good time for your first words! (She turns around and finds that Leslie has managed to crawl into the bottom drawer of the TV cabinet.) Well, look at you! Hey! You are a little bit mischievous! My gosh! (She picks her up and notices a surprise is waiting in the drawer.) Oh, you're a lot mischievous! Well, it'll dry. (Closes the drawer with her foot.) (To Leslie) Okay, you sit with your brother and sister now-who aren't there! (They both have disappeared as well.)

[Time Lapse, the babies are finally asleep. Good for Phoebe! The only problem is, Monica's apartment looks like a tornado, a hurricane, a swarm of locusts, fire, brimstone, hail, and giant man-eating, radioactive ants have torn the place apart. Needless to say, it's messy enough to cause Monica to die of shock right away. Parents with small children know what I'm talking about.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (exhausted) How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, let's just say that Krog will be fully equipped to destroy the universe again in twelve to fourteen hours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so I totally took care of the babies all by myself! I fed 'em, bathed 'em, and put 'em to bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And protected them from a tornado?
Monica enters and her jaw drops in horror.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, the babies are asleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, what, what happened here?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I did it! I took care of the babies all by myself!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But my apartment!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was the setting of Phoebe's triumph.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But the mess!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is not as important as the fact that Phoebe took care of the babies all by herself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're right, you're right I shouldn't freak out. 'Cause this is what will happen when you and I have babies! When will that be?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pause) Phoebe, would you take a look at this mess!!!!
Ross's apartment, Rachel is packing what she still has over there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (deadpan) Oh honey thank God you're home, I was getting worried.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I picked up the divorce papers. Uh, I've already signed everything and I put little 'X's where you need to sign.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, little 'X's! Great! That makes up for everything!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, I-I-you've done a lot of stupid stuff too! Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, name one stupid thing that is as stupid as this one!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, how about you flew to London to stop my wedding! Ah, how about you told me you loved me after I was already married!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Wait a minute! That was different! I did those things because I was in love with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Right!
Pause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're right. That's very different. So let's, let's just sign the papers. All right? (Sits down and Rachel keeps standing there.) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing. (Sits down.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, can we just sign please?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-hmm. (Just as Rachel finishes signing her name, Ross yanks each page out of the way.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Congratulations. (Gets up to leave.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross, we're-wait a minute. Umm, I uh, I kinda have a little confession.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know this whole marriage thing, kinda my idea.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, remember how we were too drunk to remember anything the night we were married?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And uh, yeah, I didn't really, I didn't want to say anything, but it kinda it just, it kinda kept coming back to me, and umm, remember we were in the casino and for some reason thought it would be funny to eat a lot of grapes. And uh, and I thought it would be funnier if we got married. So as a, as a compromise we decided first to get married, and then (Ross joins in) to eat a lot of grapes. So umm, sorry I got us into this mess.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So then if-if-I mean if you think about it, this is all (Pause.) your fault.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, don't push it though.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I've got to say; I know I divorce a lot of women, never thought I would be divorcing you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I always thought if you and I got married, it would be the one that stuck. And it wouldn't be a secret, and we wouldn't have our wedding dinner at Pizza Hut. (They both laugh.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did I, did I even treat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it was on the house, it was, it was a newlywed special.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That may be the most depressing thing I've heard in my life. I should probably get these to my lawyer's office.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, thanks Ross, for taking care of all of this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, no problem.
They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm gonna need a copy of those.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Totally. (Exits.)
Ending Credits
The street, Joey is messing with a car cover and still wearing the Porsche stuff. This guy playing street football catches a pass next to the car cover Joey is fooling around with.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Be careful around my Porsche!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (the same one from before approaches) Hi Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! How you doin'?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (to her friend) He has the most amazing Porsche under there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'd love to show ya, but I just tucked her in. She's sleeping. (The women both laugh) Hey uh, would you two girls like to go for a drink? (Just then the same guy with the football dives to make a catch, lands on the car cover, and collapses it. It turns out that Joey set up a bunch of boxes to make it look like a Porsche.)
End
